summary: Solid literature-grounded network with complete phenylpropanoid backbone, full TF cascade, and well-chosen monolignol branch point. Two MEDIUM-priority issues flagged: (1) a bidirectional coverage gap where Coniferaldehyde (metabolic branch point with 15 curated edges / major flux hub) is modelled correctly but the BRANCHING enzymes CCR2 and PT4CL1 whose biology explicitly documents preferential-S-reduction phenotypes lack direct edges to Lignin_SG_Ratio, so the cascade-only propagation will mispredict direction for those perturbations; (2) Phenotype activator count is 4 leaving room to add one of these two direct flux edges. One smaller MEDIUM is an excluded MYB74 -> CCOAOMT1 edge that reduces CCOAOMT1 coverage.

pathway_completeness.justification: Full phenylpropanoid entry (PAL1->Cinnamate->...->Feruloyl_CoA), G-branch (CCR2/CAD1) and S-branch (F5H1/COMT1/CAD1) present. Master-switch cascade (SND1 -> Layer-2 MYBs -> NAC156; Layer-3 MYBs -> F5H1). Auxin/ARF2.1 hormonal input included. Polymerization (LAC/PRX/ABCG29) intentionally excluded as total-lignin rather than S/G-specific; documented in literature_gap_log. Tension_Wood environmental input included. Missing: VND6 vessel-specific program, alt-splice antagonists, PLT5 timing TF - deliberate parsimony cuts.

mechanistic_depth.justification: Proper layered cascade - environment/hormone -> master TF -> Layer-2 -> Layer-3 / enzyme -> metabolite flux -> phenotype. Metabolite cascade uses sequential enzyme+substrate activator pattern so upstream KOs propagate. Only three direct phenotype edges (F5H1, MYB152, Tension_Wood + C3H1 phenomenological inhibitor), the rest flow through monomer pool. Scored 4 not 5 because C3H1 -> LSGR direct (-1) is phenomenological, not mechanistic (captures H-accumulation biology that an H-monomer node would encode more cleanly).

motif_coverage.justification: Multi-Output Scaffold (Motif 5) well applied: SND1 -> 6 Layer-2 targets, MYB92 -> 8 enzyme targets, MYB221 -> 10 repression targets, MYB156 -> 7 targets. Coherent Feed-Forward Loop (Motif 3) present: F5H1 feeds LSGR directly and via Sinapyl_Alcohol cascade (double assurance). Negative feedback (Motif 2): MYB3 -> MYB221 -| SND1 -> MYB3 (homeostasis). Perception Gate (Motif 1) not applicable in this network (no receptor-ligand pairs curated). Biosynthesis-Degradation Balance (Motif 4) NOT applied: no monolignol degradation, no F5H1 substrate turnover. This is a real gap but the curated literature doesn't provide degradation genes for S/G in Populus - it's a Step 1 literature gap rather than a BUILDER omission.

cascade_balance.justification: All node activator/inhibitor counts within 7 cap. Max: F5H1 (6 activators, 1 inhibitor), CCOAOMT1 (6, 2). Source node percentage is 25% - below the 30-50% target, but this is 'deeper cascades' rather than 'knowledge-graph', which is the healthier of the two failure modes. Residual source nodes (MYB152, MYB90, MYB161, MYB156, NF_YA11, WBLH2, CSE1, PT4CL5) lack curated upstream regulators per literature_judge_report G001 - literature-gap justified.

composite_node_handling.justification: F5H1 correctly collapses PtrCAld5H1 and PtrCAld5H2 paralogs (Wang 2012 Planta documented functional redundancy). SND1 collapses PtrWND2B/6A/6B into a composite Layer-1 master switch. MYB3/MYB20/MYB21 kept separate despite MYB46/83 orthology because their primary-paper evidence demonstrates partially distinct enzyme targets (MYB3->CCOAOMT1/COMT1, MYB21->PT4CL1). One borderline call: MYB3 and MYB20 both activate PAL1 with no distinguishing downstream - could be collapsed, but kept separate to preserve parallel evidence. Not a critical issue.

hub_completeness.justification: F5H1 coverage 9/11 (82%) - near-complete with 6 activators (MYB74, MYB90, MYB152, MYB161, NF_YA11, WBLH2) plus MYB156 repressor. Excluded CPR (P450 reductase cofactor, accessory not regulatory) and 4CL1 indirect transcriptional coupling (E023, replaced with direct cascade). SND1 coverage 9/12 (75%) - missing alt-splice antagonists (deliberate parsimony) and VND6 cross-regulation (vessel-specific). CCOAOMT1 coverage 9/19 (47%) - parsimony cuts on NAC157, VND6, MYB128, MYB121, BZIP44, NAC156 variants. Scored 4 not 5 because MYB74 -> CCOAOMT1 (curated E067) was dropped for parsimony despite being a primary-paper-supported edge.

topology_hazards.justification: No positive feedback loops. MYB3 -> MYB221 -| SND1 -> MYB3 forms a NEGATIVE feedback (secondary-wall homeostasis), stabilizing. No bypass edges violating Motif 1 (Auxin has single outgoing edge to SND1, and no receptor structure downstream requires gating). No sole-activator collapses: all nodes with perturbation-relevant phenotypes have >=2 regulators. No undocumented self-loops. Auxin -> SND1 is a single clean hormonal input.

evidence_quality.justification: Sampled 10 edges - all have specific mechanism strings (e.g., 'PtrSND1/WND2B binds SNBE sites in PtrMYB3 promoter', 'CCR reduces feruloyl-CoA to coniferaldehyde, the G/S branch-point precursor'). Evidence sentences are direct quotes from primary papers. DOIs verified to point at real journals. Two edges rely on abstract_read verification (MYB92 targets from Li 2015 PCP abstract, NF_YA11 F5H1 activation from Wei 2024 abstract) - acceptable because the abstract directly states the claim.

phenotype_audit.justification: Lignin_SG_Ratio has 4 activators (F5H1, Sinapyl_Alcohol, MYB152, Tension_Wood) and 2 inhibitors (Coniferyl_Alcohol, C3H1). Activators span key effector classes: enzyme (F5H1), metabolite (Sinapyl_Alcohol), TF (MYB152), environment (Tension_Wood). Both S-promoting and G-inhibiting arms represented. Count is at the soft cap of 5 activators, leaving 1 slot available. Scored 4 not 5 because curated CCR2 -> LSGR (E020) and PT4CL1 -> LSGR (E022) are excluded - both reflect documented preferential-S-reduction biology the cascade alone cannot model because the metabolic topology makes S-branch MORE attenuated than G-branch (geometric-mean cascade dilution).

rejected_edges_review.justification: 50 of 141 curated edges rejected. Most defensibly: (a) MYB216 edges (E055-E061) excluded per literature_judge_report G002 - Tian 2013 primary paper shows no S/G effect; (b) LAC3 / miR397a / miR166 edges - polymerization affects total lignin not S/G partitioning; (c) snd1_A2_IR / vnd6_C1_IR alt-splice antagonists - complex model, deliberate parsimony; (d) Wounding / MPK6 / LTF1 chain - specialized stress response; (e) EPSP_TF / HAT / MYB20 repressive arm - niche; (f) BZIP44 / HDA15 epigenetic modulation - niche; (g) MYB128, MYB121, MYB174, VND6 - redundant Layer-2 regulators collapsed into MYB3/MYB20/MYB21. Scored 4 not 5 because MYB74 -> CCOAOMT1 (E067) and CCR2 -> LSGR (E020) should be reconsidered for inclusion (see suggestions).

key_player_density.justification: Computed per-node ratios: CCOAOMT1 0.47, F5H1 0.82, SND1 0.75, MYB221 1.00, MYB152 1.00, MYB92 1.00, MYB156 0.78, CCR2 0.67, PT4CL1 0.56, HCT1 1.00, MYB3 1.00. All key players >= 0.40. The one 'FLAG' node (MYB216 ratio 0.00) is intentionally excluded per literature_judge_report G002. CCOAOMT1's 0.47 is the lowest in-network ratio - 10 curated edges excluded, most being review-level edges redundant with the primary-paper MYB92/NAC156/SND1 trio already included. Acceptable. Lignin_SG_Ratio phenotype node ratio 0.40 (6 of 15 curated LSGR-endpoint edges included) matches the phenotype audit finding that CCR2/PT4CL1 direct edges were dropped.

per_node_audit.comment: Rate-limiting S-branch enzyme has 6 activators (MYB74, MYB90, MYB152, MYB161, NF_YA11, WBLH2) + MYB156 repressor. Good hub representation. Note: equations corrected in iteration pre-JUDGE to remove spurious MYB92 activator (no curated edge backs it).

curated_regulators_excluded: MYB74 (E067, primary-paper supported - see suggestion S004)
per_node_audit.comment: Ratio 0.47 is lowest in-network. Most exclusions are defensible but MYB74 is a primary-paper-supported activator and would raise ratio without adding clutter.

curated_regulators_excluded: KNAT7 (E071, modest effect routed through PAL/CCR)
per_node_audit.comment: 4 activators is within the 3-5 target. Excluded CCR2 and PT4CL1 are the biggest concerns because the metabolic-cascade-only path misattenuates S (longer cascade) vs G (shorter cascade) and mispredicts CCR2-KD and PT4CL1-KO direction. See suggestions S001 and S002.
Inhibitor-only non-source with 3 repressors captures the timing-control biology. 6 downstream targets span Layer-2 cascade. Alt-splice antagonists deliberately omitted (complex composite handling).
Layer-3 source TF. All 8 curated outgoing edges included (100% coverage). Upstream regulators missing in literature (residual_gap_for_builder per literature_judge_report G001).
Broad Layer-2 activator with 8 downstream targets matching Li 2015 PCP abstract. Upstream auxin-responsive repression via ARF2.1 included.
Broad-spectrum repressor with 7 enzyme targets. UBC34 post-translational modulation dropped for parsimony (would add 2-node chain for small modulatory effect).
10 repression targets including SND1 feedback loop. 100% coverage. MYB3 upstream activation creates the negative feedback motif (homeostasis).
Entry-pathway enzyme with 2 activators and 2 inhibitors. Equations corrected in iteration pre-JUDGE to remove spurious MYB92 activator.
G/S branch-point enzyme with 4 activators + 1 inhibitor.
Has the phenomenological C3H1 -> LSGR (-1) direct edge capturing poplar-specific S/G-up on C3H KD (E021). Alternative (H-monomer node) would be more mechanistic but requires additional curation.
Environment source feeding directly into phenotype per Al-Haddad 2013 abstract (no mechanistic dissection available).

curated_regulators_excluded: PLT5 intermediate (E130+E131 compressed into direct Auxin->SND1)
per_node_audit.comment: Hormone source feeding through master switch repression (cf Johnsson 2019).
Auxin response factor repressing two cascade components (Fu 2019).
Layer-2 NAC cofactor.
Layer-2 activator; MYB221 activation creates negative feedback.

curated_regulators_excluded: MYB20->CCOAOMT1 (E044, dropped for parsimony as redundant with MYB3->CCOAOMT1)
per_node_audit.comment: Only one outgoing edge in network. Could be considered for consolidation with MYB3, but kept separate per composite-node rules (distinct primary-paper identities).
Layer-2 MYB83 ortholog with 4CL1/CCOAOMT1 specificity.

curated_regulators_excluded: MYB74->CCOAOMT1 (E067, parsimony - should be reconsidered, see S004)
per_node_audit.comment: 4 targets per Li 2018 primary paper. One additional target (CCOAOMT1) dropped for parsimony.
Single-target source - effect propagates through F5H1 and Sinapyl_Alcohol cascade (coherent feed-forward, Motif 3 but with F5H1 as the common intermediate).
Compensatory paralog; no upstream regulator curated in poplar literature.
Entry enzyme with 4 activators (at activator cap warning territory but within 7).
Shared terminal reductase for both G and S monolignol production.
G/S metabolic branch point - has 2 outgoing edges modeling the fate split.

per_pathway_audit.comment: Complete. PAL1, C4H1, PT4CL1 with proper TF regulation and repressor antagonism.
Complete with both classical path and CSE/4CL5 compensatory shortcut.
Complete. CAD1 shared reductase models G/S branching correctly.
Complete. F5H1 rate-limiting step correctly gated.

per_pathway_audit.pathway: Master-switch TF cascade (SND1 -> Layer-2 MYBs/NAC -> enzymes)
per_pathway_audit.comment: SND1 -> 6 targets (MYB3/20/21, NAC156, KNAT7, CCOAOMT1). Layer-2 MYBs regulate enzyme subsets. Layer-3 specialists (MYB152/90/161) converge on F5H1/COMT1. Repressors MYB156/MYB221/KNAT7 included. Negative feedback MYB3 -> MYB221 -| SND1 present.
missing_pieces: VND6 vessel-specific program (deliberate focus on fibre/xylem general)

per_pathway_audit.comment: Auxin and ARF2.1 repress SND1. Tension_Wood enters directly at phenotype. PLT5 intermediate and Wounding/MPK6/LTF1 stress arm omitted.
missing_pieces: PLT5 intermediate (E130, E131 - compressed into direct Auxin->SND1)
Wounding -> MPK6 -| LTF1 -| PT4CL1 (specialized stress, not constitutive)

per_pathway_audit.comment: Intentionally excluded because LAC3/PRX/ABCG29 effects are on total lignin content rather than S/G partitioning.
missing_pieces: ABCG29 monolignol transporter (not in curated repo - residual gap per G002)

per_pathway_audit.comment: No monolignol degradation enzymes in the curated repo for poplar - Motif 4 cannot be applied. Flag as literature_gap for future Step 1 updates but not a BUILDER omission.
missing_pieces: No curated monolignol / S-branch degradation genes available

suggestions.description: Add CCR2 -> Lignin_SG_Ratio (+1) direct edge to capture the preferential S-reduction phenotype of CCR2 KD that the metabolic cascade alone cannot predict (the S-branch cascade is longer so S is MORE attenuated than G under shared-precursor knockdowns, causing S/G to go UP in cascade-only when biology says DOWN).
suggestions.biological_justification: Leple 2007 Plant Cell (E020) documents that CCR KD in poplar gives 'S/G ratio significantly lower than wild type; relatively more reduced in syringyl than in guaiacyl'. This is a flux-level preferential-S biology that requires a direct phenotype edge to model correctly in the current geometric-mean cascade framework.
suggestions.implementation: Add edge CCR2 -> Lignin_SG_Ratio (+1) citing Leple 2007 and De Meester 2020 Nat Commun. Update LSGR activators list from 4 to 5. This keeps the activator count at the 5-ideal cap.

suggestions.description: Add PT4CL1 -> Lignin_SG_Ratio (+1) direct edge to capture the preferential S-reduction phenotype of 4CL1 KO (Wang 2020) that cascade-only model mispredicts for the same reason as CCR2.
suggestions.biological_justification: Wang 2020 Plant Physiology (E022) documents 4CL1 CRISPR-KO gives 30% decrease in S/G with 34% S reduction vs 5% G reduction - clear preferential-S phenomenon. Voelker 2010 antisense gives S/G decline 1.8-2.3 -> 1.1-1.4. Both support the direct edge.
suggestions.implementation: Add edge PT4CL1 -> Lignin_SG_Ratio (+1). Would raise LSGR activators to 6, above the ideal 5 cap. To stay within cap, could either (a) accept 6 activators as below the 7 hard cap, or (b) remove F5H1 -> LSGR direct and rely on Sinapyl_Alcohol cascade to carry F5H1 signal. Option (a) is simpler and preserves the F5H1 rate-limiting-direct-contribution signal.

suggestions.description: Add MYB74 -> CCOAOMT1 (+1) edge back. Li 2018 Frontiers in Plant Science lists CCOAOMT1 among the direct promoter-activation targets of PtoMYB74.
suggestions.biological_justification: Dropped for parsimony during BUILDER but the evidence (E067) is primary-paper transactivation assay, not a review-level inference. This would raise CCOAOMT1 coverage from 6 -> 7 activators (still within the 7 cap) and key-player ratio from 0.47 to 0.53.
suggestions.implementation: Add N094 MYB74 -> CCOAOMT1 (+1). Update CCOAOMT1 equation activators from 6 to 7.

suggestions.description: Consider removing MYB3 -> MYB221 edge (N046) or demoting mechanism description to reflect that the MYB46 -> MYB4 activation is an Arabidopsis-orthology claim; primary poplar evidence for the feedback loop is that MYB221 represses SND1 (included) and MYB3 is implied as upstream via MYB4/MYB46 correspondence.
suggestions.biological_justification: Tang 2015 PdMYB221 paper states 'MYB4 can be activated by MYB46, a second-level master regulator' - this is a cross-species claim, not direct poplar evidence of PtrMYB3 activating PdMYB221. The feedback loop still exists via MYB221 -| SND1 and SND1 -> MYB3 with MYB221 being expressed via some upstream we may not have fully modelled.
suggestions.implementation: Either (a) downgrade edge confidence to LOW and keep, or (b) remove and let MYB221 become is_source=true. Option (a) preferred - keeping the negative feedback loop is biologically valuable even if the direct evidence is orthology-inferred.

suggestions.description: Motif 4 (Biosynthesis-Degradation Balance) is not applied to any monolignol or CoA thioester because no monolignol degradation enzymes are curated for poplar.
suggestions.biological_justification: Step 1 LITERATURE REVIEW did not extract degradation biology for lignin precursors. Future refinement could search 'monolignol oxidation', 'coniferyl aldehyde turnover', 'lignin precursor catabolism Populus'.
suggestions.implementation: Flag for Step 1.5 re-run in a future build if degradation effects become validator-relevant.

suggestions.description: Source percentage 25% is below the 30-50% target because 8 peripheral TFs lack curated upstream regulators. Most are Layer-3 S-specific activators (MYB152/90/161, NF_YA11, WBLH2) - consistent with residual_gaps_for_builder in literature_judge_report.
suggestions.biological_justification: Not a Builder error - reflects the Populus primary-literature coverage of upstream promoter analysis for secondary-tier lignin MYBs.
suggestions.implementation: No BUILDER action required. The JUDGE accepts this as literature-gap-driven.

literature_gaps: Monolignol / S-branch aldehyde degradation enzymes (for Motif 4 Biosynthesis-Degradation Balance)
Upstream promoter-binding evidence for PtrMYB152, PtrMYB090, PtrMYB161, NF_YA11, WBLH2, MYB156 in poplar (needed to reduce source-node percentage)
PtrMYB28 poplar-specific primary paper (MYB58/63 ortholog - Layer-3 lignin-specific activator mentioned in reviews but no Populus direct-promoter study in curated repo)
PtrVNS11/12/13/14 VND/NST/SND paralog family - not curated as separate nodes (composite SND1 acceptable but fibre-vs-vessel distinction collapsed)
Single F5H paralog KD poplar phenotype (critical for CCR2/4CL1/F5H loss-of-function prediction calibration)
